helpful: Provisioned petition text and QP excerpt made the merits analysis possible without any document fetching.
Per-fee-class Term rates in statpack.json (paid ~5.4% for Term 2025) were the single most useful calibration number.
fedcourts query rows carry distribution_count, which let me compare this docket's conference history against recently granted priors.
gaps: The prompt references a per-Term 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack table, but this build of metrics/statpack.md carries no such section, so I anchored on the paid fee-class rate instead.

No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) status; it is the dominant pre-conference grant signal for paid petitions where respondents waived, and a CFR-conditioned base rate would sharpen exactly this kind of cell.

fedcourts query has no free-text or issue-area filter usable on SCOTUS rows, so pulling 'Contract Clause' or 'bill of attainder' priors from the corpus is not possible; I fell back on general granted-petition docket shapes.
notes: Forward cell; retrieval kept light (2 corpus queries, 1 MCP search) since the snapshot was fetched same-day and no BIO exists yet.
